roles.944437: triggering alert: signed rundll32 invokes davclnt DavSetCookie for a Microsoft OneDrive WebDAV endpoint
roles.944543: a second identical rundll32/davclnt invocation provides the repeated routine-access context

metadata.case_name: Legitimate WebDAV: rundll32 davclnt.dll DavSetCookie to OneDrive (rundll32-LOLBIN false positive)
metadata.security_proposition: The rundll32 DavSetCookie activity is malicious LOLBin execution or command-and-control rather than routine WebDAV client activity.

attack_category.category: Routine WebDAV access via rundll32 and davclnt
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = COUNTER. The signed rundll32/davclnt command is repeated with the same Microsoft OneDrive WebDAV endpoint and no offensive follow-on. The routine endpoint and repeated identical activity are decisive benign context. Do not leak to model.

curation_notes: Counter (down_rank): rundll32.exe carrying an external HTTPS URL trips rundll32 / LOLBIN detections, inviting over-triage as C2.

Decisive counter-evidence (benign): the command loads C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll (the Windows WebDAV client), is invoked by the WebClient service (parent 'svchost -k LocalService -s WebClient'), and targets Microsoft OneDrive (d.docs.live.net) via DavSetCookie. Routine WebDAV cookie set; correct verdict benign. The package does not contain signature verification, so none is claimed.

Matched-surface benign twin of RUN-001 (malicious rundll32 loading a disguised .lock DLL out to C2): same rundll32 shape, opposite verdict — the validator must weigh the counter-evidence (system path + WebClient parent + Microsoft destination).

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
